synthesis.summary: FCX dropped 12.59% on 2.59x relative volume with no supplied news to explain the move. A decline of this magnitude in a major copper miner typically reflects macro factors — copper price deterioration, global growth fears, tariff/trade concerns, or broad commodity selloff — but none of those drivers appear in the provided news set.

ON Semiconductor is up nearly 10% today on 2.38x normal volume with a 5-day gain of 22%, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The surge could reflect sector rotation into chip stocks, options-driven momentum, or a catalyst not captured in the provided news feed — but nothing here supports a fundamental explanation.

NOW dropped 17.57% on nearly 3x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The RSI at 34.9 and the 5-day loss of ~12% suggest sustained selling pressure, but the catalyst — whether an earnings miss, guidance cut, or large block liquidation — is not visible in the available news.
